summary: webpack-patch fetches and evaluates remote JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com paste host
details: Package `webpack-patch` impersonates the webpack ecosystem while shipping a remote-code-execution stager unrelated to webpack. When the exported middleware is invoked, index.js calls runJobA, which spawns `node lib/caller.js` as a detached child process with stdio ignored and unref'd, hiding it from the consuming application. lib/caller.js then issues an axios GET to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/QWPQX (with base64-encoded alternates https://jsonkeeper.com/b/XRGF3 and https://jsonkeeper.com/b/4NAKK stored under a fake `process.DEV_API_KEY` variable in lib/const.js) and passes the returned `cookie` field into `new Function.constructor('require', s)`, invoking it with the real `require`. The result is unpinned, unauthenticated remote code execution with full Node privileges in the installer's environment, delivered through a mutable anonymous paste host. The base64 obfuscation of destination URLs, the detached/stdio-ignored spawn, the impersonation of the webpack name, and the fetch-and-eval mechanism together constitute a deliberate dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
